title: Chocolate cream pie
subtitle: Stovetop chocolate custard in a baked pastry shell with whipped cream
excerpt: A rich, dark chocolate custard cooked on the stove, poured into a blind-baked pastry case, and chilled until set, then topped with softly whipped cream. An American dinner classic with a clean, intense chocolate flavour.
sourceNotes: Chocolate cream pie is a staple of American domestic cookery documented from the late nineteenth century. Fannie Farmer, The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book (1896), includes recipes for cream pies with stove-cooked custard fillings. The chocolate version became a fixture of American diner and church-supper menus through the early twentieth century. The distinguishing feature is the stovetop-cooked custard (not a baked filling) that sets by chilling rather than by further oven cooking.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps refrigerated for 2 days. Add the whipped cream topping just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: 200°C conventional / 180°C fan (pastry blind bake only; filling is cooked on the stove)

content.text: Chocolate cream pie has two stages: a blind-baked pastry shell and a stove-cooked chocolate custard that sets on chilling rather than baking. The custard is a standard cornflour-thickened milk custard enriched with egg yolks and dark chocolate. Cooking it past the point of initial thickening (until it bubbles and the starch is fully cooked out) is what gives the filling a clean, non-starchy flavour and a firm, sliceable set.
Press a sheet of cling film directly against the surface of the custard before chilling to prevent a skin forming. The skin won't cause harm but it will give the filling an uneven, lumpy top.

Make a shortcrust from 200 g {{plain-flour}}, 100 g cold {{unsalted-butter}}, {{fine-salt}}, and 2-3 tbsp cold water. Chill 30 minutes, roll out, and line a 23 cm pie dish. Chill 20 more minutes. Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Blind bake with baking paper and beans for 15 minutes, remove beans and paper, bake 8-10 minutes more until fully golden and dry. Cool completely.

Whisk together the {{caster-sugar}}, {{cornflour}}, and {{cocoa-powder}} in a medium saucepan. Whisk in the {{whole-milk}} gradually until smooth. Set over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly with a whisk, until the mixture thickens and begins to boil (about 8-10 minutes). Reduce the heat to low and cook for 2 more minutes, still stirring: this cooks out the starch taste. Remove from the heat. Whisk the {{egg-yolks}} in a bowl, then whisk in a ladleful of the hot custard to temper them. Pour the egg mixture back into the pan and return to low heat, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in the {{dark-chocolate}}, {{unsalted-butter}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} until smooth.

Pour the hot custard into the cooled shell. Press cling film directly against the surface to prevent a skin. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours until fully set. Top with whipped {{double-cream}} before serving.
